+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "kcp-1183-apiexport-consumer-can-t-wildcard-list-watch-its-schemas-until-the-firs",
+  "missionClass": "fixer",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "kcp: APIExport consumer can't wildcard list/watch its schemas until the first APIBinding is created",
+    "description": "APIExport consumer can't wildcard list/watch its schemas until the first APIBinding is created. Community-reported issue.",
+    "type": "troubleshoot",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Identify kcp troubleshoot symptoms",
+        "description": "Check for the issue in your kcp deployment:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods -n kcp -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kcp\nkubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kcp -n kcp --tail=100 | grep -i error\n```\nLook for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Review kcp configuration",
+        "description": "Inspect the relevant kcp configuration:\n```bash\nkubectl get all -n kcp -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kcp\nkubectl get configmap -n kcp -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=kcp\n```\nA controller using APIExports can't successfully start up and wildcard list/watch the APIs it's exporting until the first APIBinding is created.\n\n**To Reproduce**\nSteps to reproduce the behavior:\n1. Create an APIResourceSchema\n2."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Apply the fix for APIExport consumer can't wildcard list/watch its schemas…",
+        "description": "there is a problem though with the vw solution: what about watches? They have to start and then eventually show objects when the first APIBinding is created on that shard. That's tricky. We could have a fake storage for identity based requests always returning empty, or a watch without events. When\n```yaml\nhttps://$server/services/apiexport/root:default:andy/controller-runtime-example-data.my.domain/clusters/*/apis/data.my.domain/v1alpha1/widgets\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Confirm APIExport consumer can't wildcard list/watch its… is resolved",
+        "description": "Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:\n```bash\nkubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kcp -n kcp --tail=50 --since=5m\nkubectl get events -n kcp --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10\n```\nConfirm that the issue symptoms are gone."
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "there is a problem though with the vw solution: what about watches? They have to start and then eventually show objects when the first APIBinding is created on that shard. That's tricky. We could have a fake storage for identity based requests always returning empty, or a watch without events.",
+      "codeSnippets": [
+        "https://$server/services/apiexport/root:default:andy/controller-runtime-example-data.my.domain/clusters/*/apis/data.my.domain/v1alpha1/widgets"
+      ]
+    }
+  },
+  "metadata": {
+    "tags": [
+      "kcp",
+      "sandbox",
+      "app-definition",
+      "troubleshoot"
+    ],
+    "cncfProjects": [
+      "kcp"
+    ],
+    "targetResourceKinds": [],
+    "difficulty": "intermediate",
+    "issueTypes": [
+      "troubleshoot"
+    ],
+    "maturity": "sandbox",
+    "sourceUrls": {
+      "issue": "https://github.com/kcp-dev/kcp/issues/1183",
+      "repo": "https://github.com/kcp-dev/kcp"
+    },
+    "reactions": 1,
+    "comments": 25,
+    "synthesizedBy": "copilot"
+  },
+  "prerequisites": {
+    "kubernetes": ">=1.24",
+    "tools": [
+      "kubectl"
+    ],
+    "description": "A running Kubernetes cluster with kcp installed or the issue environment reproducible."
+  },
+  "security": {
+    "scannedAt": "2026-04-01T06:51:17.938Z",
+    "scannerVersion": "cncf-gen-3.0.0",
+    "sanitized": true,
+    "findings": []
+  }
+}
